pred: It seems like two-thirds of the Spanish electorate supports the Democratic party.

source: Los críticos demócratas denuncian el carácter partidista de las leyes que se han votado, ya que está claro que con ellas se pretende influir en los resultados de 2012 en los estados clave.
target_pos: Democratic critics denounce the partisan character of the laws that have been passed and they see a clear objective of influencing the 2012 results in key States.
pred: Democratic critics denounce the party-line nature of the laws that have been voted, as it is clear that with them they intend to influence the results of 2012 in key states.

source: Hemos pedido consejo a dos especialistas.
target_pos: We asked two specialists for their opinion.
pred: We consulted with two specialists.

source: Arizona fue el primer estado en presentar dicha exigencia.
target_pos: Arizona was the first to introduce such a requirement.
pred: Arizona was the first state to present such an exigency.

source: De hecho, un 11% de los ciudadanos americanos, es decir, 21 millones de personas en edad de votar, no cuentan con ningún documento de identidad con fotografía emitido por una agencia gubernamental de su estado.
target_pos: In fact, 11% of American citizens, i.e. 21 million people of voting age, do not possess a photo ID card issued by a government agency of their State.
pred: In fact, 11% of American citizens, that is 21 million people eligible to vote, do not have any photo identification document issued by a government agency in their state.

source: Los hombres de origen afroamericano tienen un índice de riesgo más elevado.
target_pos: African-American men are also more at risk.
pred: Black American men have a higher risk of death.

source: Ahora bien, el Centro Brennan considera esto último un mito y afirma que el fraude electoral es menos frecuente en los Estados Unidos que el número de personas que mueren a causa de la caída de un rayo.
target_pos: However, the Brennan Centre considers this a myth, stating that electoral fraud is rarer in the United States than the number of people killed by lightning.
pred: Now, the Brennan Center considers this last myth and claims that voter fraud is less frequent in the United States than the number of people who die as a result of a lightning strike.

source: Este fenómeno se ha extendido tras las elecciones de noviembre de 2010, que vieron el aumento de 675 nuevos representantes republicanos en 26 estados.
target_pos: This phenomenon gained momentum following the November 2010 elections, which saw 675 new Republican representatives added in 26 States.
pred: This phenomenon has spread after the 2010 November elections, which saw an increase of 675 new Republican representatives in 26 states.

source: Antes de las elecciones de 2006, ningún estado americano exigía que los electores presentaran un documento de identidad con fotografía.
target_pos: Before the 2006 elections, no US State required voters to show a photo ID card.
pred: Before the 2006 elections, no American state demanded that voters present an identity document with a photograph.

source: Estos no representan más que un 11% de los electores, lo que equivale a un 24% de los ciudadanos que votan por adelantado.
target_pos: These represented only 11% of voters, but 24% of citizens who voted early.
pred: These are not more than a 11% of the voters, which is equivalent to a 24% of those who vote early.

source: Por otra parte, esos mismos estados autorizan a los miembros de los clubs de pesca o de caza que suelen votar a los partidos republicanos a utilizar los documentos emitidos por dichos clubs.
target_pos: On the other hand, these same States allow fishing or hunting club members, who vote more Republican, to use the cards issued by these clubs when they vote.
pred: Furthermore, those same states authorize fishing or hunting clubs members to vote for Republican parties to use documents issued by said clubs.

source: Los dirigentes republicanos justificaron su política por la necesidad de luchar contra el fraude electoral.
target_pos: Republican leaders justified their policy by the need to combat electoral fraud.
pred: Republican leaders justified their policy by the need to fight against electoral fraud.

source: Antes de 2004 ningún estado exigía la prueba de ciudadanía para votar.
target_pos: Prior to 2004, no State required proof of citizenship to vote.
pred: Before 2004, no state demanded citizenship testing to vote.

source: Sin embargo, es innegable que las nuevas reglas adoptadas complicarán el ejercicio del derecho a voto en 2012.
target_pos: However, the new rules put in place will undoubtedly make it more difficult to exercise the right to vote in 2012.
pred: However, it is undeniable that the new rules adopted will complicate the right to vote in 2012.

source: De hecho, los abogados republicanos no han encontrado más que 300 casos de fraude electoral en los Estados Unidos en diez años.
target_pos: Indeed, Republican lawyers identified only 300 cases of electoral fraud in the United States in a decade.
pred: In fact, Republican lawyers have found only 300 cases of electoral fraud in the United States in ten years.

source: Pero, a menudo, la obtención del documento de identidad requerido cuesta más de cien dólares.
target_pos: And it often costs over a hundred dollars to obtain the required identity card.
pred: But, often, obtaining the required identity document costs more than a hundred dollars.

source: El Tribunal Supremo de los Estados Unidos confirmó en 2008 la constitucionalidad de la ley de Indiana.
target_pos: In 2008, the Supreme Court of the United States upheld the constitutionality of the Indiana law.
pred: The Supreme Court of the United States confirmed the constitutionality of the Indiana law in 2008.

source: Las medidas adoptadas desde 2009 lograron que la tasa de inscripción de nuevos electores cayera en 2010 en comparación con el año 2006.
target_pos: However, the measures adopted since 2009 have led to a 17% drop in the registration rate of new voters in 2010 compared to 2006.
pred: The measures adopted since 2009 resulted in the enrollment rate of new voters falling in 2010 compared to the year 2006.

source: Es demasiado pronto para afirmar con certeza si estas modificaciones legislativas a escala de sistema electoral tendrán un impacto significativo en los resultados de las elecciones presidenciales de 2012.
target_pos: It is too early to say with certainty that these legislative changes in the electoral system will have significant impacts on the outcome of the 2012 presidential elections.
pred: It is too early to definitively say whether the legislative changes on a scale of electoral system will have a significant impact on the results of the 2012 presidential elections.

source: Recomiendo, por tanto, el test a partir de los 50 años, o a partir de los 40 años si tu padre ha sufrido cáncer de próstata.
target_pos: I therefore recommend the test from age 50, or 40 if you have a direct relative who previously had prostate cancer.
pred: I recommend, by extension, the test starting from the 50s, or starting at the 40s if your father has suffered prostate cancer.

source: Por su parte, el estudio europeo mostró cierta diferencia de mortalidad entre los pacientes que se habían realizado la detección y los que no.
target_pos: Another study, this time a European one, concluded that there was a difference in mortality between patients who were screened and those who were not.
pred: In turn, the European study showed a slight difference in mortality between patients who had been screened and those who hadn't.

source: Una cosa es cierta: esas nuevas disposiciones afectarán negativamente a la tasa de participación.
target_pos: But one thing is certain: these new provisions will have a negative impact on the turn-out.
pred: One thing is certain: those new regulations will negatively affect the participation rate.

source: Por ello, las autoridades republicanas se apresuraron a extender esa práctica a otros estados.
target_pos: The Republican authorities were quick to extend this practice to other States.
pred: Therefore, republican authorities rushed to extend this practice to other states.

source: Por ejemplo, en las elecciones generales de 2004, las campañas de inscripción de los electores contribuyeron al registro de alrededor de 10 millones de ciudadanos.
target_pos: For example, during the 2004 general election, voter registration campaigns contributed to registering around 10 million citizens.
pred: For example, in the 2004 general elections, voter registration campaigns contributed to the registration of around 10 million citizens.

source: Es un motivo más que suficiente para hacer dudar a los hombres, ya reticentes de por sí, sobre si deberían hacerse o no los tests de detección.
target_pos: Enough to make already reluctant men hesitate to take screening tests.
pred: It is a more than enough reason to make men doubt whether they should do the testing.

source: En los estudios realizados en los Estados Unidos se detectó un gran índice de contagios entre los grupos de muestra, por lo que es difícil interpretar esos datos y hacer recomendaciones firmes.
target_pos: In studies conducted in the United States, there was a lot of contamination between control groups, so it is difficult to interpret the data and make firm recommendations.
pred: In the studies conducted in the United States, a large number of infections was detected between the sample groups, so it is difficult to interpret those data and make firm recommendations.

source: En el caso de los hispanos ocurría exactamente lo mismo.
target_pos: The same applied to Hispanics.
pred: In the case of the Spanish-speaking population, it happened exactly the same way.

source: Una cosa es cierta: esas nuevas disposiciones afectarán negativamente a la tasa de participación.
target_pos: One thing is certain: these new provisions will have a negative impact on voter turn-out.
pred: One thing is certain: those new regulations will negatively affect the participation rate.

source: Por supuesto, los legisladores demócratas y sus partidarios se han opuesto fervientemente a la adopción de leyes que restrinjan el registro de electores.
target_pos: Of course, Democratic legislators and their supporters vigorously opposed the adoption of laws restricting voter registration.
pred: Of course, Democratic legislators and their supporters have vehemently opposed the adoption of laws that restrict voter registration.

source: El Ministerio Fiscal de los Estados Unidos intervino para suspender las leyes más controvertidas.
target_pos: The United States Attorney General intervened to suspend the most controversial laws.
pred: The U.S. Attorney General intervened to suspend the most controversial laws.

source: Se pudo, en parte, limitar los daños.
target_pos: They were able to partially limit the damage.
pred: If possible, in part, to limit the damage.

source: El estudio, con un seguimiento a los 12 años, demostró además que existe entre el 30 y el 40% de posibilidades de padecer metástasis si no se ha realizado el test de detección.
target_pos: This study also showed, with a follow-up after 12 years, that it is between 30 and 40% more likely for metastases to occur in the absence of screening.
pred: The study, with a follow-up of 12 years, also showed that there is a 30-40% chance of metastasis if the detection test has not been completed.

source: En este sentido, los gobernadores demócratas han bloqueado varios proyectos de ley.
target_pos: Several bills were blocked by vetoes of Democratic governors.
pred: In this sense, Democratic governors have blocked several law projects.

source: El Estado de Indiana fue el primero en exigirlo.
target_pos: Indiana was the first State to impose such a requirement.
pred: Indiana was the first to demand it.

source: En efecto, un 25% de afroamericanos, un 15% de personas que ganan menos de 35.000 dólares al año, un 18% de los ciudadanos de más de 65 años y un 20% de los electores de entre 18 y 29 años no cuentan con un documento de identidad con fotografía.
target_pos: In fact, 25% of African Americans, 15% of those earning less than $35,000; 18% of citizens over 65 and 20% of voters 18 to 29 years old do not have the required photo ID card.
pred: In fact, a 25% of African Americans, a 15% of those earning less than $35,000 a year, a 18% of citizens over 65 years old, and a 20% of voters between 18 and 29 years old do not have an identity document with a photograph.

source: La clave es tomar una buena decisión una vez detectado el cáncer.
target_pos: The key is to make the right decision once cancer has been detected.
pred: The key is to make a good decision once cancer is detected.

source: Las nuevas leyes electorales exigen que los electores presenten un documento de identidad con una fotografía, además de una prueba de ciudadanía americana.
target_pos: The new election laws require voters to show a photo ID card and proof of US citizenship.
pred: The new electoral laws require voters to present an identity document with a photograph, in addition to a US citizenship test.

source: Y aún hay más.
target_pos: And that's not all.
pred: There are still more.

source: En efecto, el test de PSA presenta, en ocasiones, resultados erróneos, con falsos resultados negativos o incluso falsos resultados positivos, tras los que se decide realizar intervenciones médicas inútiles.
target_pos: Indeed, the PSA test sometimes shows erroneous results with false negative or even false positive results, which involve unnecessary medical interventions.
pred: In fact, the PSA test can sometimes produce incorrect results, with false negative or even false positive results, after which interventions in vain are decided.

source: Sin embargo, los blancos, que representan un 76% del electorado, no representan más que el 46% de los ciudadanos que votan por adelantado.
target_pos: On the other hand, 76% of voters were white but these represented only 46% of early voters.
pred: However, the white population, which represents 76% of the electorate, represents no more than 46% of voters who cast ballots early.

source: Hay cánceres agresivos y otros cánceres que son indolentes.
target_pos: There are aggressive cancers and others that are indolent.
pred: There are aggressive cancers and other cancers that are indolent.

source: Es importante tener en cuenta que, al contrario de lo que sucede en Quebec, los ciudadanos americanos no disponen de un documento de identidad universal, como la tarjeta de la Seguridad Social.
target_pos: It is important to note that, unlike Quebec, American citizens do not have a universal ID card such as the health insurance card.
pred: It is important to note that, in contrast to what happens in Quebec, American citizens do not have a universal identity document, such as the Social Security card.

source: En muchos estados los estudiantes, los electores que más votan a los candidatos demócratas, no pueden utilizar el documento de identidad con fotografía emitido por su institución.
target_pos: Students, voters considered to be voting more for Democratic candidates, are not allowed in several States to use the photo ID card issued by their institution.
pred: In many states, students, election officials who vote the most for Democratic candidates, cannot use the identity document issued by their institution.

source: Al contrario de lo que ocurre en Canadá, los estados americanos son responsables de la organización de las elecciones federales en los Estados Unidos.
target_pos: Unlike in Canada, the American States are responsible for the organisation of federal elections in the United States.
pred: In contrast to what happens in Canada, the states in America are responsible for the organization of federal elections in the United States.

source: ¿Hacerse el test o no?
target_pos: Take the test or not?
pred: Should I take the test or not?

source: Esas medidas buscan, claramente, limitar el voto hispano.
target_pos: These measures are clearly intended to limit the Hispanic vote.
pred: These measures seek, clearly, to limit the Spanish vote.
